event_blocks.summary: Zendaya, seated on a black couch in a plant-filled room and wearing a tan utility jumpsuit, begins her "Life in Looks" for Vogue by reflecting on past fashion choices, including a controversial hat look she stands by. The narrative transitions into a dynamic montage of vintage video clips and then a scrapbook presentation. She revisits her 2011 Teen Vogue Young Hollywood Party look, noting her age and early collaboration with Law Roach. A 2013 Dream Halloween photo shows her dressed as Aaliyah, a tribute she explains. Further nostalgic photos appear before she discusses a 2014 Teen Vogue Young Hollywood Party look, calling it controversial. The scrapbook then reveals her 2015 Academy Awards appearance in a white gown with braids, her first Oscars, where she humorously admits to sneaking onto the red carpet. Another 2015 page features her first Met Gala, themed 'China: Through The Looking Glass,' in a striking red and black sunburst dress, recalling her nervousness at 18. The montage returns, showing a butterfly dress, before the scrapbook highlights her 2017 Vogue US cover in a feathered Prada outfit, expressing honor and cherishing the moment with her nieces and Law Roach. Finally, a photo from 'The Greatest Showman' Sydney Film Premiere showcases a butterfly-themed one-shoulder dress, identified as an all-time favorite inspired by her film roles.

2013 Dream Halloween Photo.description: Zendaya in a red jacket, graphic tee, and loose jeans, standing on a red carpet at the 20th Annual Keep A Child Alive, Dream Halloween event. Zendaya explains this was a 'charity event,' 'Halloween themed,' where she dressed 'as Aaliyah,' noting 'old Tommy Hilfiger was her look and her thing,' and that she did her 'little swoop' as a 'tribute to the one and only'.

Vintage Video Montage.description: A multi-screen display with glitch effects showing fragmented clips of Zendaya’s past appearances, creating a nostalgic and dynamic visual transition.

Nostalgic Photos.description: A collage of younger Zendaya in various outfits, including a yellow jacket with sunglasses, a blue jacket with a striped top, and a black crop top with pink shorts, shown during a reflective moment.

Zendaya.description: Wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a plant-filled room, she expressively engages with a scrapbook, narrating her fashion journey in a clear, reflective voice. She shares personal anecdotes, such as struggling with her phone at her 'first Met' as an 18-year-old, and offers insights into her outfits, like her 'feather look' for Vogue (which she believes was Prada) and the inspiration behind her 'butterfly' dress for 'The Greatest Showman' premiere, expressing pride and honor for these moments.

2014 Teen Vogue Photo.description: Zendaya in a black jumpsuit and bowler hat, standing by a pool with purple lighting at the 12th Annual Teen Vogue Young Hollywood Party. Zendaya initially refers to this look by saying, 'I still see memes about this particular look with my hat,' and later calls it 'a controversial look'.

Scrapbook.description: A central prop on a round table, its pages are flipped by Zendaya to reveal key fashion moments from 2017, including a Vogue US July 2017 cover, a Met Gala photo (with visible text '2015 Met Gala'), and a photo from 'The Greatest Showman' Sydney Film Premiere, serving as visual cues for her detailed, personal reflections.

2011 Teen Vogue Photo.description: Zendaya in a blue patterned dress with black tights and heels, posing at the 9th Annual Teen Vogue’s Young Hollywood Party. Zendaya describes this as 'me and Law's early work together,' a 'cute little dress' for her 'Teen Vogue party' when she was 'like 15 years old,' concluding, 'I think I did alright'.

2015 Met Gala Photo.description: An image of Zendaya in a red and black dress with a sunburst design and a headpiece, posing on the red carpet at the Met Gala, with text '2015 Met Gala, 'China: Through The Looking Glass'' displayed on the scrapbook page. This photo is the subject of Zendaya's reflection on her 'first Met,' where she recalls being 18, nervous, and attending with 'Fausto Puglisi,' and worrying about how she would get home without a phone.

2015 Academy Awards Photo.description: An image of Zendaya in a white off-the-shoulder gown with long braids, standing on a red carpet beside an Oscar statue, with text '2015 87th Annual Academy Awards' displayed on the scrapbook page. This photo is the subject of Zendaya's anecdote about her 'first Oscars experience,' where she humorously recounts sneaking onto the red carpet after getting ready all night in an 'amazing Vivienne Westwood dress.'

2017 Met Gala Photo.description: Zendaya in a striking black dress with a sunburst design and matching headpiece, as depicted on a scrapbook page displaying '2015 Met Gala' text. Zendaya reflects on this as her 'first Met' as an 18-year-old, sharing an anecdote about the practical challenge of carrying her phone, and expressing pride in the look, which she felt was 'very fitting for that moment.'

2017 Sydney Film Premiere Photo.description: Zendaya in a vibrant, one-shoulder dress with a monarch butterfly wing print, from 'The Greatest Showman' premiere in Australia. Zendaya identifies this as 'one of my all-time favorites,' explaining that the butterfly theme was inspired by the films she was doing, embodying a 'costumey' idea.

2017 Vogue US Cover Photo.description: Zendaya on the cover of Vogue US July 2017, wearing a feathered, sheer outfit (which she believes was Prada), with the headline 'Fly High' and text identifying her as 'Actor, Activist, It Girl.' Zendaya recounts being 20 at the time, still doing her Disney show, feeling 'very, very honored,' and cherishing the involvement of her nieces and Law Roach, appreciating the 'beautiful texture' of the look.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Zendaya, seated on a black couch in a warmly lit, plant-filled room, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back. She engages with an open book on a round wooden table, surrounded by decorative elements like colorful flowers, an hourglass, and a bell. Zendaya's voice immediately begins, reflecting on a past look, stating, "I still see memes about this particular look with my hat. But you know what? I stick by it." She then introduces herself, "Hello Vogue, I'm Zendaya and this is my Life in Looks," as the camera alternates between close-ups of her expressive face and wider shots of the setting. An upbeat, slightly retro background music track begins as the scene transitions into a dynamic montage of vintage-style video screens displaying fragmented clips of her past appearances, overlaid with glitch effects. Zendaya's voice returns, asking, "Alright, shall we begin?" as the narrative shifts to a scrapbook-style presentation. As pages flip, revealing a 2011 photo of her at the 9th Annual Teen Vogue’s Young Hollywood Party in a blue patterned dress, she comments, "Yeah, this is me and Law's early, early work together. I needed a cute little dress to go to my Teen Vogue party. Was a big deal. I'm like what, like 15 years old. So, I think I did alright." The scrapbook continues to a 2013 image from the 20th Annual Keep A Child Alive, Dream Halloween event, showing her in a red jacket and jeans. Zendaya explains, "This was a charity event, it was Halloween themed, so you were supposed to come dressed as somebody or something and I went dressed as Aaliyah, 'cause old Tommy Hilfiger was her look and her thing and so this was my interpretation of that. I did my little swoop and that was my tribute to the one and only." Additional nostalgic photos appear, including a younger Zendaya in a yellow jacket with sunglasses, and another in a blue jacket with a striped top. The segment concludes with a 2014 image from the 12th Annual Teen Vogue Young Hollywood Party, where she wears a sleek black jumpsuit and bowler hat, standing by a pool with purple lighting, as Zendaya's voice is heard saying, "This, this is a controversial look." Throughout, the atmosphere blends personal reflection with a curated visual journey through her fashion evolution, emphasizing her growth and style milestones, all underscored by her direct, conversational commentary.

The segment opens with Zendaya, dressed in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a warmly lit, plant-filled room. She engages with a Scrapbook placed on a round table, flipping through its pages that showcase her past fashion moments. As she views an image, she reflects aloud in a clear, conversational voice, recalling how she and 'Law' (likely her stylist) thought a particular hat look was 'chic' and that she'd 'wear it again,' even suggesting 'Law might have this hat.' The Scrapbook then displays a page from 2015, featuring her at the 87th Annual Academy Awards in a white off-the-shoulder gown with long braids, standing beside an Oscar statue on a red carpet. Zendaya narrates this as her 'first Oscars experience,' humorously admitting she 'snuck onto the red carpet' after staying up all night getting ready in an 'amazing Vivienne Westwood dress.' She smiles and gestures as she recounts the memory. The Scrapbook transitions to another 2015 page, showing her at the Met Gala themed 'China: Through The Looking Glass,' in a striking red and black sunburst dress and headpiece. Zendaya identifies this as her 'first Met,' recalling she was 18, went with 'Fausto Puglisi,' and was very nervous, wondering how she'd get picked up or get home since phones weren't allowed. Throughout, Zendaya's expressive reactions—smiling, nodding, and looking up reflectively—tie her spoken anecdotes to the dynamic visual display of her fashion history, set against the cozy, nostalgic backdrop.

The video opens with Zendaya, dressed in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a cozy, plant-filled room. She engages with a scrapbook on a round table, gesturing expressively as she reflects on her fashion journey in a clear, conversational voice. Flipping through pages, she first discusses a red carpet moment where she wore a striking black dress with a sunburst design and a matching headpiece, visible on a scrapbook page that displays '2015 Met Gala' text. Zendaya recounts this as her 'first Met' as a young 18-year-old, sharing an anecdote about struggling to find a place for her phone and expressing pride in the look, which she felt was 'very fitting for that moment.' The scene transitions to a dynamic montage of her past appearances, including a close-up of a vibrant butterfly dress, before returning to the scrapbook. She then highlights her Vogue US July 2017 cover, featuring her in a feathered, sheer outfit by what she believes was Prada, with text 'Fly High' and 'Zendaya: Actor, Activist, It Girl.' She recalls being 20 at the time, still doing her Disney show, feeling 'very, very honored,' and cherishing the presence of her nieces and Law Roach, appreciating the 'beautiful texture' of the cover look. Finally, the scrapbook displays a photo from 'The Greatest Showman' Sydney Film Premiere, where she dons a vibrant, one-shoulder dress with a monarch butterfly wing print. Zendaya identifies this as 'one of my all-time favorites,' explaining that the butterfly theme was inspired by the films she was doing, embodying a 'costumey' idea. The atmosphere remains warm and introspective, blending her personal storytelling with glamorous highlights, all framed by the natural light and greenery of her living space.

event_blocks.summary: A Vintage Video Montage, complete with glitch effects and fragmented clips, opens the narrative, showcasing Zendaya's past appearances, including a striking image in a butterfly-wing dress and a scene from 'The Greatest Showman.' Zendaya's voice connects to these visuals, labeling the 'Greatest Showman' look as 'drama' and recalling the butterfly dress day as having 'tons of butterflies' on the carpet, which she interpreted as 'a sign.' The scene then shifts to Zendaya, dressed in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a plant-filled room, interacting with a Scrapbook on a round table. Flipping through its pages, she first reveals the '2018 Vanity Fair’s 'Women in Hollywood' Event,' displaying a photo of her in a bold, colorful Marc Jacobs suit and black hat. Gesturing expressively, she reflects on this 'meme'd' look, which she 'loved' for its 'extreme proportions' and having worn it 'casually' 'straight off the runway,' deeming it 'fun and dramatic.' A distinct page-turning sound signals the transition to the '2019 Met Gala, 'Heavenly Bodies: Fashion & The Catholic Imagination'' look: a shimmering silver chainmail-like gown with a dramatic cape and a short, red bob wig. Zendaya declares this 'one of my all-time favorite Met looks,' explaining Law's 'Joan of Arc inspired thing' concept. Real footage of her walking the red carpet in this outfit, amidst photographers and attendees, is shown before returning to Zendaya, who continues thoughtfully, hands clasped, describing the gown as 'so heavy because of the beading' but 'beautifully constructed,' mentioning 'Sacha,' and the difficulty of wearing heels, all within an intimate, reflective atmosphere enhanced by soft lighting and natural decor.

Vintage Video Montage.description: A multi-screen display with glitch effects showing fragmented clips of Zendaya’s past appearances, including a butterfly-wing dress and a scene from 'The Greatest Showman,' creating a nostalgic and dynamic visual transition. Zendaya's voice directly references the 'Greatest Showman' and the butterfly dress, linking her commentary to these specific visuals.

Zendaya.description: Wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a plant-filled room, interacting with a scrapbook and reacting expressively to past images. She speaks in a clear, conversational tone, reflecting on the 'Greatest Showman' look as 'drama,' recalling 'tons of butterflies' on the carpet with her butterfly dress, describing her Marc Jacobs suit as 'meme'd,' 'fun,' and having 'extreme proportions' that she wore 'casually' off the runway. She then introduces her 'all-time favorite' Met Gala look, explaining its 'Joan of Arc inspired' concept by 'Law,' and detailing its weight ('so heavy because of the beading') and beautiful construction, mentioning 'Sacha' and her experience with heels.

Scrapbook.description: A visual representation of a photo album with pages flipping to reveal key fashion moments from 2018 and 2019, including the Vanity Fair 'Women in Hollywood' Event and the 2019 Met Gala, with text labels and photos of Zendaya in bold, colorful, and metallic outfits. A distinct page-turning sound is heard as Zendaya transitions between discussing the Marc Jacobs suit and the Met Gala look.

2019 Met Gala Photo.description: Zendaya in a silver, chainmail-inspired gown with a high collar, shoulder armor, and a flowing cape, paired with a short red bob wig, shown both in the scrapbook and in real red carpet footage at the 'Heavenly Bodies: Fashion & The Catholic Imagination' Met Gala. Zendaya declares this 'one of my all-time favorite Met looks,' explaining its 'Joan of Arc inspired' concept by 'Law,' and describing it as 'so heavy because of the beading' but 'beautifully constructed,' also mentioning 'Sacha' and the challenge of wearing heels.

2018 Vanity Fair Photo.description: Zendaya in a vibrant, color-blocked Marc Jacobs suit with a black hat, posing at the Vanity Fair 'Women in Hollywood' Event, displayed in the scrapbook with the year and event title clearly labeled. Zendaya describes this outfit as 'another meme'd' look that she 'loved' for its 'extreme proportions' and how she 'casually' wore it 'straight off the runway,' calling it 'fun and dramatic.'

Zendaya's voice immediately connects to these visuals, reflecting on the 'Greatest Showman' look as 'drama' and recalling that the day she wore the butterfly dress, 'there was tons of butterflies' on the carpet, which she saw as 'a sign.' The scene transitions to Zendaya, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a cozy, plant-filled room. She interacts with a Scrapbook placed on a round table, flipping through pages that reveal key fashion moments. The first page shown is labeled '2018 Vanity Fair’s 'Women in Hollywood' Event,' displaying a photo of Zendaya in a bold, colorful Marc Jacobs suit with a black hat. She gestures expressively as she reflects on the image, noting it was 'another meme'd' look that she 'loved' for its 'extreme proportions' and how she 'casually' wore it 'straight off the runway,' describing it as 'fun and dramatic.' A distinct page-turning sound accompanies the transition to the next page, which showcases her '2019 Met Gala, 'Heavenly Bodies: Fashion & The Catholic Imagination'' look: a shimmering silver chainmail-like gown with a dramatic cape and a short, red bob wig. Zendaya declares this 'one of my all-time favorite Met looks,' explaining that 'Law had had this idea of doing a Joan of Arc inspired thing.' The video cuts to real footage of her walking the red carpet in that outfit, surrounded by photographers and attendees, before returning to Zendaya, who continues to speak thoughtfully, her hands clasped, as she looks down at the scrapbook, describing the gown as 'so heavy because of the beading' but 'beautifully constructed,' mentioning 'Sacha,' and reflecting on the challenge of wearing heels. The atmosphere remains intimate and reflective, with soft lighting and natural decor enhancing her personal storytelling.

event_blocks.summary: Zendaya, seated on a black couch in a cozy, plant-filled room wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, reflects on her fashion journey through a scrapbook. She recalls struggling with platforms and a heavy dress, finding it worth it for the character, and mentions her upcoming co-chair role for which her outfit is still undecided. The scrapbook reveals a 2019 'Euphoria' page with photos of her as Rue Bennett, highlighting costume details like her worn-down Chucks, and a 2019 Vogue US cover shot by Tyler Mitchell, which she describes as her second Vogue cover. Transitioning to 2019, she discusses her 'Spider-Man: Far From Home' premiere look, an Armani red sequined top and skirt, initially overthinking it but finding it perfect and theme-appropriate. Next, she shows her bold green strapless gown from the 71st Annual Primetime Emmy Awards, her first time attending, noting her use of semi-permanent red hair dye and her affinity for emerald green. For the 2020 Critics’ Choice Awards, she highlights a pink latex crop top and skirt with long braids, and a special Tom Ford breastplate customized with a scanner. She then recalls winning her first Emmy in 2020, wearing a black and white polka-dot Armani gown, feeling like a princess and humorously considering closing her computer during the virtual ceremony. The 2020 72nd Annual Primetime Emmy Awards saw her in another voluminous Armani gown, a 'princess dress' that held its structure. For the 2021 93rd Annual Academy Awards, she wore a vibrant yellow Valentino strapless gown, a 'fun, generous, and thoughtful' collaboration with Pierpaolo, inspired by a shared reference and a 'beautiful, almost neon yellow' color. Real red carpet footage shows her in the yellow gown, humorously recalling sudden wind and a 'leaf blower' joke. She recounts the 78th Annual Venice International Film Festival look, a ruched beige gown for 'Dune,' feeling it was 'very Dune' with its landscape and color palette resemblance. A white Rick Owens gown for the London Screening of 'Dune' is described as wrinkle-free and allowing movement, feeling 'futuristic or otherworldly.' The 2021 CFDA Fashion Awards featured a red strapless top and flared pants, a collaboration with Law Roach, where Iman presented her with an award, a 'really, really special' and 'warmest moment.' For the 2022 74th Annual Primetime Emmy Awards, she wore a black Valentino gown, her first all-black real gown, and proudly held her Emmy. The 2023 World Premiere of 'Dune: Part Two' featured a futuristic metallic suit, a concept she proposed to Law, which fit 'like a glove' but caused her to feel 'really lightheaded' due to heat conduction. The 2024 96th Annual Academy Awards look was a shimmering, floral-patterned, one-shoulder gown, described as 'old Hollywood' and 'keeping it simple' after the 'Dune' press tour. A 2024 Vogue US May Issue cover shoot with Annie Leibovitz is highlighted, where Zendaya collaborated on poses inspired by movement and dance, working with couture garments. She concludes by drawing a stylized 'Z' in the scrapbook, identifying it as a shooting star, and expresses gratitude to Vogue for the 'life in looks' segment before closing the scrapbook. The segment ends with production credits and the VOGUE logo, accompanied by gentle acoustic guitar music.

2019 Vogue US Photo.description: Zendaya in a shimmering, textured dress with a high neckline, photographed by Tyler Mitchell, with text '2019 Vogue US, June Issue, Photographed by Tyler Mitchell' displayed on the scrapbook page.

Zendaya.description: Wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch, interacting with a scrapbook and reacting expressively to past images displayed in a scrapbook format. She speaks reflectively about her past, expresses gratitude to Vogue, and bids farewell with a warm tone.

2019 Euphoria Photo.description: Zendaya in a black long-sleeve top and dark pants, sitting on a bed with long, wavy hair, holding a phone, with text '2019 HBO’s 'Euphoria,' Rue Bennett' displayed on the scrapbook page.

Scrapbook.description: A visual representation of a photo album with pages flipping to reveal key moments from 2023 and 2024, including the 2023 World Premiere of 'Dune: Part Two' with a futuristic metallic suit, and the 2024 96th Annual Academy Awards with a shimmering floral gown, both accompanied by text labels and photos of Zendaya in dramatic and fashion-forward outfits. The sound of a page turning is heard as Zendaya interacts with it.

2019 Los Angeles Film Premiere Photo.description: Zendaya in a red sequined top with a black sequined skirt and long train, standing on a red carpet at the 'Spider-Man: Far From Home' premiere, with the event backdrop featuring logos like Audi and United Airlines.

2019 Emmy Awards Photo.description: Zendaya in a green strapless gown with a high slit, posing on a purple carpet at the 71st Annual Primetime Emmy Awards, with the Emmy logo visible in the background and her wavy auburn hair styled elegantly.

2020 Emmy Awards Photo.description: Zendaya in a black and white polka-dot gown with a silver top, holding a golden Emmy statuette, with her hair styled in an elegant updo, displayed in the scrapbook with the text '2020 72nd Annual Primetime Emmy Awards.'

2020 Critics’ Choice Awards Photo.description: Zendaya in a shiny pink latex crop top and matching skirt, with long braids, posing on the red carpet in front of a backdrop with 'FIJI Water,' 'The CW,' and '#SEE HER' logos.

2021 Academy Awards Photo.description: Zendaya in a vibrant yellow strapless gown with a cutout detail and a dramatic train, standing on a red carpet against a geometric backdrop with Oscar statues, displayed in the scrapbook with the text '2021 93rd Annual Academy Awards.'

2021 Academy Awards Red Carpet Footage.description: Real-time footage of Zendaya walking confidently in her yellow gown on the red carpet, with photographers capturing her from multiple angles, set against a backdrop of repeating Oscar statues and geometric patterns.

2021 Venice International Film Festival.description: Zendaya in a ruched beige gown with a high slit and a large emerald necklace, posing on a red carpet, displayed in the scrapbook with the text '2021 78th Annual Venice International Film Festival.'

2021 London Screening, 'Dune' Photo.description: Zendaya in a white gown with an asymmetrical neckline and long train, standing on a red carpet against a cosmic-themed backdrop, displayed in the scrapbook with the text '2021 London Screening, 'Dune''.

2021 CFDA Fashion Awards Photo.description: Zendaya in a vibrant red strapless top and matching flared pants, with her hair in long braids, posing on a black carpet at the CFDA Fashion Awards, displayed in the scrapbook with the text '2021 CFDA Fashion Awards'.

2022 Emmy Awards Photo.description: Zendaya in a black strapless gown with a large bow at the waist, posing on a blue carpet with 'emmys' and 'NBC' logos in the background, shown in both front and back views in a split-screen format.

Futuristic Costume Triptych.description: Three side-by-side images in the scrapbook showing variations of the same metallic, sci-fi costume: one with a helmet, one with a purple-lit background, and one in a desert-like setting, highlighting design details and poses.

2022 Emmy Awards Red Carpet Footage.description: Real-time footage of Zendaya in the same black gown, holding the Emmy statuette, smiling, with the blue 'emmys' backdrop visible.

2023 World Premiere, 'Dune: Part Two'.description: Zendaya in a futuristic, metallic, form-fitting suit with exposed midriff, high heels, and intricate silver armor, standing on a red carpet with a 'Dune' backdrop, displayed in the scrapbook with the text '2023 World Premiere, 'Dune: Part Two''.

2024 96th Annual Academy Awards.description: Zendaya in a shimmering, floral-patterned, one-shoulder gown, standing on a red carpet beside an Oscar statue, with text '2024 96th Annual Academy Awards' displayed on the scrapbook page.

2024 Vogue US Cover Photo.description: Zendaya on the cover of Vogue US May 2024, lying amidst greenery wrapped in a large red rose, with the headline 'Zendaya' and the tagline 'What does my future look like?', photographed by Annie Leibovitz.

Vintage Video Montage.description: A multi-screen display with glitch effects showing fragmented clips of Zendaya’s past appearances, including a butterfly-wing dress and a scene from 'The Greatest Showman,' creating a nostalgic and dynamic visual transition.

2015 Met Gala Photo.description: Zendaya in a red and black dress with a sunburst design and a headpiece, posing on the red carpet at the Met Gala, with text '2015 Met Gala, 'China: Through The Looking Glass'' displayed on the scrapbook page.

2017 Met Gala Photo.description: Zendaya in a black dress with a sunburst design and matching headpiece, posing on the red carpet at the Met Gala, with text '2015 Met Gala, 'China: Through The Looking Glass'' displayed on the scrapbook page (note: the year in the text is inconsistent with the visual context of 2017).

Production Credits.description: A series of black screens with white text listing the director, cinematographer, editors, producers, stylist, set designers, and other crew members involved in the video's creation, presented in a clean, minimalist font.

VOGUE Logo.description: The iconic 'VOGUE' wordmark in bold, white, serif font centered on a black background, serving as the final visual element of the segment, directly referenced by Zendaya in her speech as she thanks 'Vogue'.

2014 Teen Vogue Photo.description: Zendaya in a black jumpsuit and bowler hat, standing by a pool with purple lighting at the 12th Annual Teen Vogue Young Hollywood Party.

2017 Sydney Film Premiere Photo.description: Zendaya in a one-shoulder dress with a monarch butterfly wing print, standing on a red carpet at the Sydney Film Premiere of 'The Greatest Showman,' with text '2017 Sydney Film Premiere, 'The Greatest Showman'' displayed on the scrapbook page.

2015 Academy Awards Photo.description: Zendaya in a white off-the-shoulder gown with long braids, standing on a red carpet beside an Oscar statue, with text '2015 87th Annual Academy Awards' displayed on the scrapbook page.

2017 Vogue US Cover Photo.description: Zendaya on the cover of Vogue US July 2017, wearing a sheer, feathered outfit with the headline 'Fly High' and text identifying her as 'Actor, Activist, It Girl,' photographed by Mario Testino.

2018 Vanity Fair Photo.description: Zendaya in a vibrant, color-blocked suit with a black hat, posing at the Vanity Fair 'Women in Hollywood' Event, displayed in the scrapbook with the year and event title clearly labeled.

2013 Dream Halloween Photo.description: Zendaya in a red jacket, graphic tee, and loose jeans, standing on a red carpet at the 20th Annual Keep A Child Alive, Dream Halloween event.

Nostalgic Photos.description: A collage of younger Zendaya in various outfits, including a yellow jacket with sunglasses, a blue jacket with a striped top, and a black crop top with pink shorts, shown during a reflective moment.

2019 Met Gala Photo.description: Zendaya in a silver, chainmail-inspired gown with a high collar, shoulder armor, and a flowing cape, paired with a short red bob wig, shown both in the scrapbook and in real red carpet footage at the 'Heavenly Bodies: Fashion & The Catholic Imagination' Met Gala.

2011 Teen Vogue Photo.description: Zendaya in a blue patterned dress with black tights and heels, posing at the 9th Annual Teen Vogue’s Young Hollywood Party.

raw_segments: The video opens with Zendaya, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a cozy, plant-filled room. She gestures expressively as she reflects on past experiences, sharing in a clear, reflective voice, "I don't know, it was something about a mixture of like wearing these platforms and like the heaviness of the of of of my of my dress. I was struggling. I was like, I need to sit down. But it it was all worth it. The hair also just completed the character." She also mentions a future event, stating, "This year I was invited to be a co-chair. I have no idea what I'm wearing yet, so I can't spoil it for you guys. We're still figuring it out." The camera alternates between medium shots and close-ups, capturing her thoughtful expressions as she interacts with a scrapbook placed on a round table. The scrapbook flips to a 2019 page featuring her role as Rue Bennett in HBO’s 'Euphoria,' displaying a photo of her sitting on a bed, long hair down, holding a phone, followed by a collage of four images showing her in various emotional states and outfits, including a red hoodie and a white t-shirt with 'MY RELIGION' printed on it. Zendaya identifies the character, saying, "This is Miss Rue Bennett. I'm very grateful and honored to play this character," and credits costume designer Heidi, highlighting the importance of details like her "personal Chucks" that she's "worn down quite a bit." The scrapbook then transitions to a 2019 Vogue US June Issue photo, where she is shown in a shimmering, textured dress with a high neckline, photographed by Tyler Mitchell. She describes this as "My second Vogue cover," adding, "We shot this with Tyler on the beach. I'm very grateful to have a Vogue cover. It's a big deal." Throughout, Zendaya reacts with subtle smiles and nods, engaging with the memories in the warm, intimate setting, with soft natural light filtering through a large window, surrounded by potted plants and floral arrangements.

The video opens with Zendaya, dressed in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a cozy, plant-filled room. She engages with a scrapbook on a round table, flipping through pages that showcase key moments from 2019. The first page reveals a photo of her at the Los Angeles Film Premiere of 'Spider-Man: Far From Home,' where she's seen in a striking red sequined Armani top with a black sequined skirt and a long train on a red carpet. Zendaya audibly reflects on the dress, admitting she was "overthinking it a little bit" initially, but it ended up being "perfect" and "theme dressing" inspired by the red and black Spider-Man suit. The scene transitions to a close-up of this photo, emphasizing her poised stance. The scrapbook then turns to the next page, displaying her at the 71st Annual Primetime Emmy Awards in a bold green strapless gown with a high slit on a purple carpet. Zendaya gestures expressively, noting it was her "first time" at the Emmys and describing the look as "sexy." She also mentions using "semi-permanent hair dye to make my hair red" for the press tour and being "really into emerald green at the time." A close-up highlights her wavy auburn hair and the intricate green dress. The segment concludes with Zendaya continuing to interact with the scrapbook, her hands moving over the pages as she speaks, surrounded by warm lighting and decorative elements, creating an intimate and reflective atmosphere.

Zendaya, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, is seated on a black couch in a warmly lit, plant-filled room, gesturing expressively as she focuses on a scrapbook resting on a round stone table. The scrapbook flips open to reveal a photo of her at the 2020 Critics’ Choice Awards, dressed in a striking pink latex crop top and matching skirt with long braids, posing confidently on the red carpet in front of 'FIJI Water,' 'The CW,' and '#SEE HER' logos. Zendaya's clear, conversational voice narrates, identifying it as the Critics' Choice Awards and highlighting the 'really special' Tom Ford breastplate. The scene then cuts to real red carpet footage of her smiling and posing in the bold outfit, before returning to the scrapbook. Zendaya continues to explain how the breastplate was customized, describing a 'hair dryer' like scanner that uploads to a computer, remarking on the surprising ease of the 'technology people' involved. A distinct paper rustling sound accompanies the scrapbook flipping to a new page, displaying her at the 2020 Emmy Awards, holding a golden Emmy statuette in a black and white polka-dot gown with a silver top and an elegant updo. Reacting with amusement and pride, Zendaya's voice identifies this as 'The Emmys,' the 'first time that I won,' and reflects on her thoughts during the virtual event, humorously considering slamming her computer shut if something went wrong, like the Wi-Fi going out. The intimate room, decorated with vibrant floral arrangements and greenery, enhances the reflective atmosphere as the scrapbook serves as a personal timeline of her 2020 achievements.

Zendaya, seated on a black couch in a cozy, plant-filled room, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, speaks reflectively about past events. She engages with a scrapbook placed on a round stone table, gesturing expressively as she recalls moments. She points to a photo labeled '2020 72nd Annual Primetime Emmy Awards,' showing her in a black and white polka-dot gown holding an Emmy statuette. Zendaya describes this as a 'very special night' spent in her 'own living room' with family, wearing an 'Armani dress' that felt 'like a princess dress,' noting its 'voluminous' skirt 'still stands up' and 'holds its structure,' making it 'unforgettable.' An audible page turn signals a transition to a new spread titled '2021 93rd Annual Academy Awards,' featuring her in a vibrant yellow strapless gown with a cutout detail and a dramatic train, standing on a red carpet against a geometric backdrop with Oscar statues. Zendaya identifies this as 'another Oscar's moment,' crediting 'Valentino' and 'Pierpaolo' as a 'fun, generous, and thoughtful collaborator.' She explains they had a 'shared reference' and chose a 'beautiful, almost neon yellow' color, noting the dress and hair were 'quite simple' to reflect 'shared inspiration,' and the dress was 'really long.' The scene transitions to a close-up of Zendaya in the same yellow gown, followed by a multi-panel collage showcasing different angles of the dress and a close-up of her diamond necklace. The video then cuts to real red carpet footage of Zendaya walking confidently in the yellow gown, her hair flowing, as photographers capture her from multiple angles. Zendaya humorously recalls that despite no initial wind, it suddenly appeared upon her arrival, prompting a joke from 'Law' about needing a 'leaf blower.' The atmosphere throughout is celebratory and reflective, blending personal memory with glamorous public moments.

Zendaya, dressed in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, is seated on a black couch in a cozy, plant-filled room. She animatedly recounts a humorous anecdote about a past red carpet event, saying, "everyone was like, did you really? Like, did you really? Were you really on the carpet with a leaf blower? Like, no. Oh my god, no." She explains that while they aim for dramatic looks, sometimes "the wind just wanted to visit us in that moment and it worked out." A distinct 'clack' sound is heard as she interacts with a scrapbook on a round stone table, flipping through pages showcasing key moments from 2021. She points to the first page, revealing a photo of her at the 78th Annual Venice International Film Festival in a ruched beige gown with a high slit and an emerald necklace. She describes it as her "first Venice Film Festival with Dune," an example of "theme dressing," noting she chose the dress years prior and how it "feels very Dune" to her, referencing its landscape, color palette, shell-like appearance, and resemblance to a "still suit." After a soft 'thump' sound, she turns the page to a photo from the London Screening of 'Dune,' where she wears a dramatic white gown with an asymmetrical neckline. She identifies it as "a Rick Owens number," expressing her excitement and revealing she was "in the middle of shooting Euphoria during this press tour, shooting some really intense scenes." The warmly lit room, with natural light, potted plants, and a vase of red flowers, enhances the intimate atmosphere as Zendaya shares personal insights with reflective and expressive gestures.

The video opens in a cozy, plant-filled room with soft lighting and lush greenery, where Zendaya, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, is seated on a black couch. She interacts with a scrapbook placed on a round table, flipping through pages that showcase key fashion moments from 2021. As she turns a page, a photo of her at the 2021 London Screening of 'Dune' appears, showing her in a striking white gown with an asymmetrical neckline and long train on a red carpet against a cosmic-themed backdrop with the word 'DUNE' illuminated in gold. Zendaya's voice describes the gown's construction, noting how its fabric 'doesn't wrinkle' and allows her to 'sit in it, move in it' without disrupting its 'beautiful shape.' She muses that the design, possibly by Rick Owens, feels 'very futuristic or otherworldly,' appreciating how the piece 'holds its shape in a really beautiful way' with an 'effortless' yet 'intentional' and 'thoughtful' drape. The scene transitions to real footage of Zendaya posing confidently at the 'Dune' event, her hair styled in a sleek updo and makeup featuring bold, smoky eyes, before cutting back to the scrapbook. The next page reveals her at the 2021 CFDA Fashion Awards, where she is seen in a vibrant red strapless top and matching flared pants with long braids. Zendaya identifies this as 'the CFDA Fashion Awards,' calling it 'another very special look' and a 'collaboration with Law Roach.' She explains that her stylist, Law, had a 'very specific reference' for the red outfit, describing it as a 'bandeau kind of top and then kind of column skirt.' Throughout, Zendaya reacts expressively to the images, gesturing with her hands, her face animated with nostalgia and pride, as the clearly labeled scrapbook pages evoke warm, reflective memories.

The video opens in a cozy, plant-filled room with warm lighting, where Zendaya, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, is seated on a black couch. She expressively interacts with a scrapbook placed on a round stone coffee table, which holds a vase of red and yellow flowers, an hourglass, and a teacup. As she flips through the scrapbook, images of her past appearances are displayed in a stylized, bordered format. She discusses a close-up of her in a red outfit, calling red a "power color" and noting how beautiful everyone looks in it. The scrapbook then shows a full-body shot of her in a red pantsuit at the 2021 CFDA Fashion Awards, and she animatedly recalls her excitement because Iman presented her with the award, describing Iman as "so kind and beautiful and warm," and the moment as "really, really special," including a "biggest hug" and "warmest moment." With a cheerful "The Emmys again, we're back!", she turns the page to reveal the '2022 74th Annual Primetime Emmy Awards' photo, showing her in a green strapless gown on the red carpet. Her expressions shift as she reflects on the contrast with previous virtual events, realizing she had to "get up on that stage in front of like actual people," unable to "close the computer" or "blame it on bad Wi-Fi." She candidly admits she was "so like tense the whole time" and wished she could have enjoyed it more until it was over, as the camera alternates between medium shots of her and close-ups of her thoughtful and engaging face.

The video opens with Zendaya, dressed in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, seated on a black couch in a warmly lit, plant-filled room, interacting with a scrapbook placed on a round table adorned with flowers, candles, and a small hourglass. As the scrapbook flips, Zendaya reflects on a split-screen image from the 74th Annual Primetime Emmy Awards, showing her in a black strapless gown with 'emmys' and 'NBC' logos. She describes the dress as "Valentino, yet again," a reference to an old design, noting its "mermaid shape" and "bodice with the line and the little bow," and recalling it was her "first time wearing like a real gown that was all black." The page turns to show her proudly holding the Emmy statuette. The narrative then progresses to a 2023 World Premiere of 'Dune: Part Two,' where a photo of Zendaya in a futuristic, metallic, form-fitting suit appears. She reacts with animated enthusiasm, stating, "This suit, everybody knows it," and recounts sending the idea to "Law" (her stylist), asking, "what if we wore this for the premiere?" She describes his initial skepticism and her determination, followed by a discussion with one of the suit's original makers about potential fit challenges due to differing proportions, mentioning, "My elbow is a little bit lower or my whatever then it doesn't it doesn't." The scrapbook then displays a triptych of three different versions of the same futuristic costume, highlighting its design evolution. Throughout, Zendaya's expressions shift from reflective to excited, her voice conveying her journey and the significance of these moments, all set against a backdrop of gentle ambient music.

The video opens with Zendaya, seated on a black couch in a warmly lit, plant-filled room, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back. She gestures expressively as she speaks, her attention drawn to a scrapbook on a round table. She recounts the challenges of a futuristic metallic suit, explaining how 'the metal conducts and holds onto heat' and made her feel 'really like lightheaded,' despite fitting 'like a glove.' The scrapbook is open to a page featuring a photo of her in this metallic, form-fitting suit with exposed midriff and intricate silver armor, labeled '2023 World Premiere, 'Dune: Part Two'.' As she reflects on her initial thought that it was 'a bad idea' but she 'went out there and I did it,' the video cuts to real-time footage of her walking confidently on a red carpet in the same ensemble, posing with hands on hips against a pink 'Dune' backdrop. The scene transitions back to her in the room, where she continues to reflect, flipping the scrapbook page with an audible click. A new image is revealed: a photo of her in a shimmering, floral-patterned, one-shoulder gown on the red carpet at the '2024 96th Annual Academy Awards,' with an Oscar statue visible. She reacts with animated gestures, smiling and nodding, stating, 'It fit me really well and I felt beautiful in it,' and describing 'the material, that shiny thing' and its 'really strong, like, well-made zipper,' clearly engaged in recounting her fashion journey in the intimate, reflective setting.

The video transitions from Zendaya's elegant red carpet appearance in a shimmering, floral-patterned, one-shoulder gown at the 2024 96th Annual Academy Awards. Her voiceover explains this look was "just kind of taking it easy, going old Hollywood and just keeping it simple" after "all these very heightened looks during the Dune press tour." The scene then shifts to a cozy, plant-filled indoor setting where Zendaya, in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, is seated on a black couch. She interacts with a scrapbook on a round stone table, flipping through pages that display images from her 2023 Dune: Part Two premiere and her 2024 Academy Awards look. As she turns to a 2024 Vogue US May Issue cover featuring her lying amidst greenery, wrapped in a large red rose, with the headline 'Zendaya' and the tagline 'What does my future look like?', she states, "This is my most recent Vogue cover. This was really special because I got to shoot with Annie." Reacting expressively with hand gestures, Zendaya describes Annie Leibovitz as "so fun to shoot with," noting her "beautiful references for poses" and inspiration from "movement and dance." She further explains Annie's specificity and the challenge of "figuring how to move in these looks," some of which were couture garments that "hadn't gone down the runway yet," expressing concern about damaging them. She humorously imitates Annie's energetic direction, concluding that they "just tried to have fun and give these garments a different feeling with movement." With gentle, reflective background music playing, Zendaya then picks up a pen and begins to draw a stylized 'Z' in the scrapbook, revealing, "I made this signature when I was like 13," and finally identifying it as "a shooting star," suggesting a personal reflection on her image and legacy in the warm, intimate, plant-filled environment.

The video opens with Zendaya, her hair pulled back and wearing a tan utility jumpsuit, seated on a black couch in a warmly lit, plant-filled room. She smiles and gestures expressively as she interacts with a large scrapbook placed on a round stone table adorned with vibrant floral arrangements. Reflecting on the images, she states, "and we're just gonna be, we're gonna be okay with that." She then looks directly at the camera with a warm, reflective expression, expressing gratitude: "Thank you so much Vogue for doing this with me. This has been my life in looks. And I hope to see you all soon." A distinct page turn sound accompanies her closing the scrapbook, revealing a brief glimpse of a photo. Gentle, reflective acoustic guitar music then fades in as the scene transitions to a black screen with white production credits, displayed in a clean, minimalist font. The credits list key roles such as Director Vivian Kim, Director of Photography Tom Marchese, Editors Boris Khaykin and Louis Lalire, and other crew members including Producers Gigi Chavarria and Naomi Nishi, Stylist Law Roach, and Set Designers Eric Hersey and Maelina Hassel. The credits continue with roles like Assistant Editor, Post-Production Manager, and Senior Post-Production Management, followed by acknowledgments for images from HBO, Art Partner / Trunk Archive, and the 72nd Emmy Awards. The music continues softly throughout the credits, which culminate in the iconic 'VOGUE' logo in bold white letters on a black background, signaling the end of the segment, with the music fading out. The atmosphere is intimate and polished, blending Zendaya's personal reflection with professional recognition.
